title: Expression and Purification of recombinant Bst DNA polymerase (Bst)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"77ghv","text":"Bst is a type I DNA polymerase with strong strand displacement activity isolated from Geobacillus stearothermophyllus (previously Bacillus steathermophylus). Bst enzyme is a gold standard in isothermal nucleic acid amplification diagnostic techniques, especially in Loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP). LAMP is a low-cost technique, provides a visual detection (when combined with pH indicators) and does not require the use of thermal cyclers. Also, Bst combined with thermostable reverse transcriptase can amplify RNA templates, in a technique known as RT-LAMP. RT-LAMP is useful for RNA virus and transcript detection and can be employed in circumstances that require mass production of diagnostic tests or limited availability of resources. This protocol shows the expression and purification procedure by FPLC of the Bst polymerase for its implementation in diagnostic techniques such as end-point colorimetric and real time fluorometric LAMP and RT-LAMP.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":86,"length":31},{"style":"italic","offset":130,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"biib8","text":"During the process of protein purification maintain all samples that contain the protein of interest in a cold environment to avoid protein degradation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Preparation of chemically competent BL21 (DE3) cells harboring pKJE7 plasmid.

Take BL21(DE3) cells harboring pKJE7 plasmid from a frozen glycerol stock using a bacterial inoculating loop and inoculate 3 mL, LB liquid medium with 30 µg/mL, of chloramphenicol . Incubate Overnight at 180 rpm, 37 °C,

Inoculate 1 mL, overnight culture in 100 mL, LB medium with 30 µg/mL, chloramphenicol and incubate at 180 rpm, 37 °C, 3 hours . Monitor the cell growth by measuring the optical density (OD) at 600 nm and remove the cells from incubation when the OD reaches 0.3 to 0.4.
Chill the cell culture On ice for 00:10:00 and centrifugate the cells at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

Gently resuspend the cell pellet in 8 mL, of TFBI solution pre-chilled and incubate On ice for 00:45:00 . Centrifugate the cells at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . TFBI Solution Composition A B 1 Potassium acetate 30 mM 2 Rubidium chloride 100 mM 3 Calcium chloride 10 mM 4 Manganese chloride 50 mM 5 Glycerol 15% v/v Adjust to pH 5.8 with 1M acetic acid and sterilize by filtration.

Gently resuspend the cell pellet in 2.5 mL, of TFBII solution pre-chilled and incubate On ice for 00:05:00 . TFBII Solution Composition A B 1 MOPS 10 mM 2 Rubidium chloride 10 mM 3 Calcium chloride 75 mM 4 Glycerol 15% v/v Adjust to pH 6.5 with 1M sodium hydroxide and sterilize by filtration.
Prepare aliquots of 50 µL, of competent cells using microcentrifuge tubes previously chilled on an ice bath. Place the aliquots on dry ice until frozen and store at -80 °C .
Transformation of chemically competent BL21 (DE3)/pKJE7 cells with the pColdI-Bst plasmid, the expression vector for the large fragment of the Bacillus stearothermophilus DNA polymerase (Bst).

Add 1 µL, of plasmidic DNA of 100 ng/µL, pColdI-Bst expression vector to 50 µL, of competent cells BL21 (DE3)/pKJE7. For the transformation procedure go to step #1 . The pColdI-Bst plasmid require 100 µg/mL, carbenicillin as selective agent and the pKJE7 plasmid require 30 µg/mL, chloramphenicol . Use LB medium supplemented with both antibiotics as selective media.
Transformation of chemically competent BL21 (DE3) cells with pKJE7 plasmid .

Add 1 µL, of plasmidic DNA consisting of the pKJE7 plasmid from the Chaperone Plasmid Set Takara Bio Inc. Catalog #3340 to 50 µL, of competent cells Chemically Competent E. coli One Shot™ BL21(DE3) Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #C600003 . Mix the cells gently and incubate On ice for 00:20:00 .
Transfer the cells to a heat block at 42 °C and incubate for 00:00:53 .
Transfer the cells immediately to an ice bath and incubate On ice for 00:05:00 .

Add 250 µL, of SOC medium at room temperature to the transformed cells and incubate at 225 rpm, 37 °C, 1 hour . SOC medium composition A B 1 Tryptone 2% 2 Yeast extract 0.5% 3 NaCl 10 mM 4 KCl 2.5 mM 5 MgCl2 10 mM 6 MgSO4 10 mM 7 Glucose 20 mM Adjust to pH 7 and sterilize by filtration.

Plate 25 µL, of transformed cell culture onto LB agar with the corresponding selective agent. Incubate the plates Overnight at 37 °C . The pKJE7 plasmid requires 30 µg/mL, chloramphenicol as selective agent.
Select a single colony of transformed cells and inoculate in 3 mL, Luria-Bertani (LB) medium supplemented with the selective antibiotic. Incubate Overnight at 180 rpm, 37 °C, .
Centrifugate the cell culture at 10000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 500 µL, LB meduim .
Add 500 µL, of 30% v/v glycerol , mix by pipetting up and down and store at -80 °C .
Preparation of bacterial cultures for Bst expression.
Inoculate 5 µL, glycerol stock of BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst or BL21(DE3)/pKJE7 cells in 5 mL, culture medium (LB or TB) supplemented with seletion agent(s). Incubate Overnight at 200 rpm, 37 °C, .

Inoculate 500 µL, overnight culture in 50 mL, culture medium with antibiotic(s) . Use LB or TB according to the medium used for the overnight culture. Incubate 200 rpm, 37 °C, for approximately 03:00:00 . Monitor the cell growth by measuring the optical density (OD) at 600 nm and remove the cells from incubation when the OD is between 0.6.
Small-scale Bst expression under different induction conditions.

Induce the expression of the Bst under different conditions. Each treatment should be evaluated in triplicate. For example: Strain [IPTG] Temperature Medium 1 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7* 0.5 mM 16°C LB 2 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7* 0.5 mM 37°C LB 3 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7* 0.5 mM 16°C TB 4 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0 mM 16°C LB 5 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0.1 mM 16°C LB 6 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0.5 mM 16°C LB 7 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 1.0 mM 16°C LB 8 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0 mM 37°C LB 9 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0.5 mM 37°C LB 10 BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst 0.5 mM 16°C TB * BL21(DE3)pKJE7 strain is used as negative expression control.
Incubate at 16 °C or 37 °C according to each treatment at 180 rpm, °C, for 16:00:00 .
Analysis of Bst expression.
Analyze all clarified supernatant samples by Tricine-SDS-PAGE electrophoresis through a 8% polyacrylamide gel. Load 100 µg, protein sample per well. Tricine-SDS-PAGE. Nature Protocols.

Measure total protein concentration by measuring absorbance at 280 nm in a NanoDrop spectrophotometer. NanoDrop™ One UV-Vis Spectrophotometer (spectrophotometer, Thermo Scientific, ND-ONE-W, Sample Volume (Metric): Minimum 1µL; Spectral Bandwidth: ≤1.8 nm (FWHM at Hg 254 nm); System Requirements: Windows™ 8.1 and 10, 64 bit; Voltage: 12 V (DC); Wavelength Range: 190–850 nm, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/ND-ONE-W )
Centrifugate the cell cultures at 6000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Discard the supernatant, remove all the liquid and leave the cell pellet as dry as posible.

Weigh the centrifugation tube with the cell pellet (total weight). Weigh the empty tube prior centrifugation and subtract it to the total weight to calculate the weight of the cell pellet and hence the biomass produced.

Resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL, lysis buffer A (LB-A) (pre-chilled). Lysis buffer A (LB-A) composition A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 EDTA 0.5 mM 3 2-mercaptoethanol 10 mM 4 Tergitol NP-40 0.1% v/v 5 Tween-20 0.1% v/v 6 PMSF 3 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.

Disrupt cells by ultrasonication at an amplitude of 40%. Apply five cycles of 00:00:15 on and 00:00:30 off. Place the tubes On ice while processing. Ultrasonic Processor (130-Watt Ultrasonic Processor, Cole-Parmer, ML-04714-52, https://www.coleparmer.com/p/cole-parmer-130-watt-ultrasonic-processors/44347 )
For protein clarification, incubate the supernatant at 60 °C for 00:20:00 in a water bath and centrifugate at 14500 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes . Recover the clarified supernatant and place it On ice .
Centrifugate at 6000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes . Recover the supernatant (soluble protein fraction) and discard the pellet.
Select the best conditions for protein expression according to the results analysis (biomass, total protein production and electrophoretic profile).
Expression of recombinant Bst.

Inoculate 10 µL, glycerol stock of BL21(DE3)/pKJE7/pColdI-Bst expression cells in 20 mL, LB medium supplemented with 100 µg/mL, carbenicillin and 30 µg/mL, chloramphenicol . Incubate Overnight at 180 rpm, 37 °C, .

Place the inoculum On ice for 00:30:00 and then add 0.5 millimolar (mM), IPTG for induction. Do not add any additional inducers. For expression of the chaperones contained in pKJE7 plasmid, the basal expression is enough to promote correct Bst enzyme folding.
Incubate at 180 rpm, 16 °C, 16 hours for recombinant protein expression.
Sample preparation. Keep all protein samples On ice during the purification process to avoid degradation.

Dilute the clarified supernatant with saline buffer at a radio of 1:1. Saline buffer composition (SB). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 NaCl 100 mM 3 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Filter the clarified supernatant through a 0.45 µm membrane.
Immobilized metal affinity chromatography (Ni 2+ -IMAC).
Desalting step.
Heparin affinity chromatography.
Purified Bst enzyme concentration and formulation.
Soluble protein fraction recovery and clarification.
Centrifugate at 6000 x g, 4 °C, 12 minutes to harvest cells. Discard the supernatant ensuring to remove all the liquid and leave the cell pellet as dry as posible.

Weigh the centrifugation tube with the cell pellet (total weight). Weigh the empty tube prior centrifugation and subtract it to the total weight to calculate the weight of the cell pellet and hence the biomass produced.

Resuspend the cell pellet in 50 mL, lysis buffer A (LB-A) (pre-cooled). If neccesary, defroze the cell pellet in an ice bath before adding the lysis buffer. Lysis buffer A composition (LB-A) A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 EDTA 0.5 mM 3 2-mercaptoethanol 10 mM 4 Tergitol NP-40 0.1% v/v 5 Tween-20 0.1% v/v 6 PMSF 3 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.

Disrupt cells by ultrasonication with an ultrasonic processor at an amplitude of 40% applying pulses of 00:00:10, of ultrasonication and 00:00:10, of pause during 00:04:00 . Place the sample On ice and keep it cold while processing. 750-Watt Ultrasonic Processor (CPX750, Cole-Parmer, ML-04711-60, https://www.coleparmer.com/p/cole-parmer-500-and-750-watt-ultrasonic-processors/16401 )
Centrifugate at 11000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes . Recover the supernatant (soluble protein fraction) and discard the pellet.
Incubate the supernatant in a water bath at 60 °C for 00:20:00 for protein clarification.

Inoculate 10 mL, overnight culture in 1 L, LB medium with 100 µg/mL, carbenicillin and 30 µg/mL, chloramphenicol . Incubate at 200-220 rpm, 37 °C, 3 hours . Monitor the cell growth by measuring the optical density (OD) at 600 nm and remove the cells from incubation when the OD reaches 0.6.
Centrifugate at 11000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes . Recover the clarified supernatant and discard the pellet. Place the supernatant On ice or store at 4°C until use.
Load the diluted fraction onto a 150 mL Superloop (Cytiva). Store at 4 °C until use.

Connect a HisTrap HP 5mL Cytiva Catalog #17524801 to a FPLC system. ÄKTA pure (Protein purification system, Cytiva, 29046665, https://www.cytivalifesciences.com/en/us/support/products/akta-pure-150-l-29046665 )

Equilibrate the column with 8 column volumes (CV) of washing buffer-AI (WB-AI) at a flow of 2.5 mL/min. Washing buffer-AI composition (WB-AI). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 NaCl 100 mM 3 Imidazole 10 mM 4 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Connect the Superloop charged with the protein fraction and load the sample onto the column at a flow of 2.5 mL/min.

Wash the column with 10 CV of 2% elution buffer-AI (EB-AI) at a flow of 2.5 mL/min. Elution buffer-AI composition (EB-AI). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 NaCl 100 mM 3 Imidazole 500 mM 4 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Elute the proteins by passing 5 CV of 100% EB-AI through the column using a flow of 2.5 mL/min.
Analyze all recolected fractions by a 8% Tricine-SDS-PAGE gel electrophoresis. Load 10 µL, protein sample per well.
Pool all elution fractions carrying the recombinant Bst protein. Store at 4 °C until use.
Connect a HiPrep 26/10 Desalting Column Cytiva Catalog #17508701 to the FPLC system.

Wash the column with 2.5 CV of Mili-Q water. Then, equilibrate the column with 2 CV of desalting buffer-A (DB-A). For both steps use a flow of 10 mL/min. Desalting buffer-A composition (DB-A). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 KCl 20 mM 3 EDTA 1 mM 4 DTT 1 mM 5 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Wash the column with 2 CV of DB-A for protein elution at a flow of 10 mL/min.

Analyze all collected fractions by qualitative Bradford assay using the Quick Start™ Bradford 1x Dye Reagent Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #5000205 .. Pool the fractions with higher protein concentration.
Connect a HiTrap Heparin HP affinity column Cytiva Catalog #17040701 to the FPLC system.
Load the pool of desalted fractions onto a 150 mL Superloop (Cytiva). Store at 4 °C until use.

Equilibrate the column with 10 CV of washing buffer-AII (WB-AII) at a flow of 2 mL/min. Washing buffer-AII composition (WB-AII). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 EDTA 1 mM 3 DTT 1 mM 4 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Connect the Superloop charged with the protein fraction and load the sample onto the column at a flow of 2 mL/min.
Wash the column with 5 CV of WB-AII at a flow of 2 mL/min.

Elute proteins by washing the column with a linear gradient of 10 CV of elution buffer-AII (EB-AII). Use a flow of 2 mL/min. Elution buffer-AII composition (EB-AII). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 50 mM 2 KCl 1 M 3 EDTA 1 mM 4 DTT 1 mM 5 PMSF 1 mM Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Analyze all collected fractions by Tricine-SDS-PAGE electrophoresis through a 8% polyacrylamide gel. Load 10 µL, protein sample per well.
Pool all elution fractions carrying the recombinant Bst protein. Store at 4 °C until use.

Load the purified Bst enzyme pool onto a dialysis membrane (pre-hydrated). Place the membrane into a beaker with precooled storage buffer-A (SB-A) at a ratio 1:50 (v/v). Storage buffer-A composition (SB-A). A B 1 Tris-HCl pH 7.5 10 mM 2 KCl 50 mM 3 EDTA 0.1 mM 4 DTT 2 mM 5 Glycerol 50% v/v Prepare the buffer with Milli-Q water and adjust to pH 7.5. Store at 4°C.
Dialyze Overnight at 4 °C with slow agitation.

Recover the dialized protein, load it onto an Amicon Ultra-15ML - 30 kDa cutoff centrifugal filter. Concentrate until a concentration equal or higher than 1 mg/mL . Amicon Ultra-15 (PLTK Ultracel-PL membrane, 15 ML - 30 kDa cutoff, Millipore, UFC903024, https://www.merckmillipore.com/MX/es/product/Amicon-Ultra-15-Centrifugal-Filter-Unit,MM_NF-UFC903024?ReferrerURL=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bing.com%2F&bd=1 ) Monitor protein concentration measuring absorbance at 280 nm using a NanoDrop spectrophotometer. NanoDrop™ One UV-Vis Spectrophotometer (spectrophotometer, Thermo Scientific, ND-ONE-W, Sample Volume (Metric): Minimum 1µL; Spectral Bandwidth: ≤1.8 nm (FWHM at Hg 254 nm); System Requirements: Windows™ 8.1 and 10, 64 bit; Voltage: 12 V (DC); Wavelength Range: 190–850 nm, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/ND-ONE-W )
Determine final protein concentration by measuring absorbance at 280 nm in a NanoDrop spectrophotometer.
Prepare aliquots of 50 µL, of concentrated Bst enzyme .
Add 0.1 % (v/v), triton X-100 to the enzyme aliquots and store at -20 °C .

Analyze the final Bst enzyme formulation by Tricine-SDS-PAGE electrophoresis through a 8 gel. Load 3 µL, protein sample per well. Load 3 µL, protein ladder Precision Plus Protein™ Unstained Protein Standards Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1610363 ..

Analyze the electrophoresis gel by densitometry using the Image Lab 6.1 Software (Bio-Rad) . Determine protein concentration for each Bst enzyme aliquot analyzed using the protein ladder as weight standard. The protein ladder Precision Plus Protein™ Unstained Protein Standards Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1610363 iincludes three reference bands: the 50 KDa with 750 ng, the 20 KDa and 100 KDa bands with 150 ng each per 10 µL of the protein ladder mix.
Load the sample onto the column at a flow of 5 mL/min.
Store the cell pellet at -80 °C until use (just in case that the purification step is not performed immediately after expression).
Once the culture reaches an OD600 of 0.6, incubate the cell cultures On ice for 00:20:00 before adding the inducer (IPTG).
Wash the column with 10 CV of WB-AI at a flow of 2.5 mL/min.
creator: Diana A Tapia-Sidas
